digest.top_line: SF-SABR (N=1687): 2-yr LC 90-93%, median OS 40mo primary NSCLC / 51mo oligomets — largest multi-institution single-fraction dataset to date.
digest.tldr: Thoracic and prostate dominated today. SF-SABR pooled data (ESTRO 2026) offer the most mature efficacy and toxicity picture for single-fraction lung SABR yet. AUA 2026 consensus tips staging toward PSMA PET over ePLND in intermediate-risk prostate, with morbidity data now quantifying the tradeoff.

sites.intro: AUA 2026 consensus formalizes PSMA PET as the preferred staging tool over ePLND in intermediate-risk disease, backed by NPV data and a growing morbidity literature.

studies.name: ePLND vs PSMA PET staging (AUA 2026)
studies.tldr: AUA 2026 consensus: PSMA PET NPV ~96% may safely replace ePLND for staging in intermediate-risk prostate cancer.
details: 🔍 Yaxley et al (BJUI 2019): 1253 men with primary staging Ga-PSMA PET/CT; 47.7% of LN metastases were outside standard ePLND template boundaries
📊 PSMA PET NPV ~96% for LNI; intermediate-risk pts with negative PSMA PET may safely omit PLND (missed LN likely below ePLND detection threshold anyway)
⚠️ No Level 1 evidence for ePLND oncological benefit; RCTs have not shown consistent BCR outcome improvements from PLND

details.text: ⚠️ ePLND morbidity burden from systematic review (Clinckaert, Cancers 2022)
subdetails: Lower limb lymphedema: 0-14% after RP + PLND
After PLND + salvage pelvic node RT: 19-29% lymphedema, 2-22% also genital
DVT/PE risk 6-10x increased with PLND vs no PLND (Tyritzis J Urol 2015, N=3544)

details: 🔗 SWOT analysis (Roberts et al, PCAN 2024): ePLND strengths (histopath staging, tumor burden reduction) increasingly offset by PSMA-PET integration enabling targeted/sentinel LND and post-op pelvic RT planning

🔍 Risk-stratified conclusions: omit PLND in intermediate risk if PSMA PET negative; GG3 use nomograms adjunctively; high-risk individualize with shared decision-making re: adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT tradeoffs

verdict.rationale: Convergent AUA 2026 evidence that PSMA PET NPV ~96% and absent Level 1 benefit data challenge routine ePLND as a staging requirement.
verdict.audience: Localized prostate cancer, intermediate-to-high risk, primary staging pre-RP

open_questions: Does omitting ePLND in PSMA-PET-negative high-risk pts compromise oncologic outcomes long-term?
Optimal nomogram threshold for GG3 to guide PLND vs imaging-only staging
Role of targeted/radioguided sentinel LND as hybrid alternative to routine ePLND

sites.intro: Largest pooled SF-SABR dataset to date; toxicity profile favorable but AE capture incomplete across sites.

studies.name: Single-fraction SABR for primary NSCLC and pulmonary oligometastases (ESTRO 2026 pooled analysis)
studies.tldr: SF-SABR (N=1687): median OS 40mo primary NSCLC, 51mo oligomets; 2-yr LC 90-93%; G3+ AEs 2.9%.
details: 🔍 Pooled analysis, 3 institutions (Peter Mac, Cleveland Clinic, Roswell Park); 1200 primary NSCLC + 487 pulmonary oligomets treated with single-fraction SABR.
📊 Median PFS 30mo (primary NSCLC) and 11mo (oligomets); 2-yr LC 90-93% across both cohorts.
📊 AEs (primary NSCLC only, n=789; Roswell Park excluded): G2+ 15.7% (124/789), G3+ 2.9% (23/789).
⚠️ Single-arm pooled series; no randomized comparator vs multi-fraction SABR. AE capture incomplete (2/3 sites, n=789/1687).
🔗 CHISEL (TROG 09.02) is the only prior randomized single vs multi-fraction SABR trial for peripheral NSCLC; this is the largest multi-institution SF-SABR dataset to date.

verdict.rationale: Single-arm pooled series; no randomized comparator vs multi-fraction SABR. AE data from 2/3 sites only.
verdict.audience: Inoperable primary NSCLC or pulmonary oligomets (lung-limited)

open_questions: Randomized non-inferiority vs multi-fraction SABR needed for practice adoption
Tumor size and location thresholds defining single-fraction eligibility
LC and OS durability beyond 5 years

Conclusions
• For intermediate risk disease PLND may safely be omitted if PSMA PET is negative for LNI,
missed LN are small and may be missed in ePLND as well
• In GG3 disease nomograms can be of additional use
•
For high-risk disease, the decision is individual and should be discussed with the patient at
length including the possibility that an adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT may increase side effects
If PLND is to be performed, then it should be in extended fashion

Inge Geraerts, Steven Joniau 2*0 and Wouter Everaerts 12.-O
• 0-14% lower limb lymphedema following Radical prostatectomy
with PLND
• 0-9% after pelvic lymph node radiotherapy
• 19-29% after PLND and salvage pelvic node radiotherapy, with
2-22% also developing genital lymphedema
• The lack of a uniform definition and standardized diagnostic
criteria for lower limb and genital lymphedema hampers the
accurate estimation of their true prevalence
Lymphadenectomy may cause harm

PERSPECTIVE
OPEN
1f) Chock for updates
Clinical
Is extended pelvic lymph node dissection REALLY required for
staging of prostate cancer in the PSMA-PET era?
Matthew J. Roberts 123, John W. Yaxley 134, Johan Stranne, Inge M. van Oort and Derya Tilki C89.10
Strengths
•Confirm metastases not detected by imaging (false-negatives)
• Accurate histopathological staging (false-positives)
• Total tumour burden reduction via excision (if nodal metastases
present)
Opportunities
•Incorporation with PSMA-PET imaging for targeted (Radioguided/
Sentinel) LND
• Improved patient selection considering prevalence of metastatic
disease (higher nomogram cut-off)
Weaknesses
• Limitation of anatomical template (missed metastases outside
template)
• Limitation of histopathology (may not detect all metastatic cells)
•Associated morbidity/complications, impact on health and quality of
life
• Increased intra-operative time
Threats
• Absent Level 1 evidence for significant oncological benefit
• Increasing demand on health services to limit low-value care/
interventions
• Surgeon proficiency/training
• Earlier use of androgen receptor signalling inhibitors and other
medications (pN status less relevant)
Alternative care pathways instead of routine eLND
PSMA-PET for staging may prompt a plan for post-operative pelvic lymph node radiotherapy,
limiting morbidity such as lymphedema, especially in the absence of a prior eND.
PCAN, 2024

Summary/Future Directions
• Low incidence of clinically significant AEs
(G3 + in 2-3%
• Local Control estimates 90-93% at 2 years
• Primary NSCLC: Median PFS 30 months and
median OS 3.5 years.
• Pulmonary Oligometastases: Median PHS 11
months and OS >4 years
• Isolated Local/Locoregional failure very
uncommon
